title: Award-Winning Active Players
description: You are given three tables: players, match results, and awards. Return usernames of players who are 'active', have won at least one match (result = 'win'), AND have received at least one award. Order alphabetically.
inputFormat:
players(id, username, status)
matches(id, player_id, result)
awards(id, player_id, award_type)
outputFormat: One username per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use JOIN on both matches and awards. Include DISTINCT and ORDER BY username. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT p.username FROM players p JOIN matches m ON p.id = m.player_id JOIN awards a ON p.id = a.player_id WHERE p.status = 'active' AND m.result = 'win' ORDER BY p.username;
hint: Join players with both matches and awards, filter on status = 'active' AND result = 'win', then use DISTINCT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE players (id INTEGER, username TEXT, status TEXT); CREATE TABLE matches (id INTEGER, player_id INTEGER, result TEXT); CREATE TABLE awards (id INTEGER, player_id INTEGER, award_type TEXT); INSERT INTO players VALUES (1,'Alice','active'),(2,'Bob','inactive'),(3,'Charlie','active'),(4,'Diana','active'); INSERT INTO matches VALUES (1,1,'win'),(2,1,'loss'),(3,2,'win'),(4,3,'win'),(5,4,'loss'); INSERT INTO awards VALUES (1,1,'MVP'),(2,3,'Rookie'),(3,2,'Veteran');
